summary: Standardize rust-diffforge focused transcript insertion on Enigo native Unicode text output instead of clipboard paste or app-local manual SendInput code.

review_items.summary: Both registered transcript insertion commands use the shared Enigo text helper so focused insertion follows Enigo's text/key_sequence path with native Unicode input where the platform supports it.
The older insert_transcribed_text command no longer uses clipboard paste fallbacks or custom app-owned Windows SendInput batching.
Microphone capture, local/cloud transcription, transcript cleaning, transcript length limits, and widget visibility behavior remain unchanged.
No database schema, migration, SQL policy, hosted API, auth, billing, or cross-project contract change is required.

patches.summary: Route the older transcript insertion command through the shared Enigo insertion helper and remove platform-specific paste/manual insertion helpers.
Keep the shared Enigo helper on Keyboard::text and align insertion error copy with native insertion behavior.
Remove app-owned Windows keyboard input structs/constants that are no longer needed after standardizing on Enigo.
Remove the now-unused SendInput declaration from the Windows user32 extern block.
Rename focused insertion widget status copy from typing to inserting.

compatibility.notes: The command names and cleaned transcript input contract remain the same; only the native insertion backend changes.

tests.summary: Validate Tauri commands and native text insertion integration.
